 {"id":"bd-sv6v","title":"Implement retry with exponential backoff","description":"Retry with exponential backoff for transient failures.

Function: retry_with_backoff(cmd, max_attempts, base_delay)

Algorithm:
- Start with base_delay (default 1s)
- On failure: delay = base * 2^(attempt-1)
- Add jitter: +/- 25% randomization
- Continue until success or max_attempts

Use cases:
- API calls that timeout
- Rate limit recovery
- Network glitches

Implementation logs warnings on each retry, returns final exit code.

Acceptance: Works for common failure patterns, respects max attempts, jitter prevents thundering herd.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:43:23.01899538-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T19:27:24.053201995-05:00","close_reason":"Added retry_with_backoff (exp backoff + jitter) and used for gh api GraphQL/rate_limit; fixed governor grep portability","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sv6v","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:15.633158875-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-szcn","title":"Auto-install flock dependency for ru review","description":"User request: when ru review needs flock, auto-install it cross-platform (similar UX to other deps).

Implement:
- Add installer helper for flock with package-manager detection.
- If interactive and allowed, prompt (or auto if RU_AUTO_INSTALL_DEPS=1) and run install.
- If non-interactive, fail with clear instructions.

Acceptance:
- On macOS with brew, offers/installs flock.
- On Linux, offers/installs util-linux via available package manager.
- ShellCheck clean; bash -n clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-05T09:03:32.524126314-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-05T11:19:30.931807003-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-05T11:19:30.931807003-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented flock auto-install + wired into review/state/parallel; docs updated","comments":[{"id":1,"issue_id":"bd-szcn","author":"ubuntu","text":"Regression: flock auto-install is not present in current ru; re-implement prompted/auto install","created_at":"2026-01-05T15:57:43Z"},{"id":2,"issue_id":"bd-szcn","author":"ubuntu","text":"Auto-install flock changes were overwritten on main; re-implement","created_at":"2026-01-05T16:14:46Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-t3mp","title":"Real unit tests for driver interface layer","description":"Test unified driver interface.

Functions to test:
- detect_review_driver(): Auto-detect available driver

- driver_capabilities(): Query driver capabilities
- driver_start_session(), driver_stop_session()
- driver_send_to_session(), driver_get_session_state()

- Driver detection priority (ntm > local)
- Graceful fallback to local
- Interface consistency across drivers
- Capability reporting

-{"id":"bd-taoo","title":"Write state management and resume tests","description":"Implements detailed tests for state management, resume, and artifact capture.

## Parent Epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Test File
scripts/test_state_management.sh

## Purpose
Verify state persistence works correctly for resume/restart functionality. Critical for handling interruptions gracefully.

## State File Tests

### State File Creation
- Test state file created at correct location
- Test state file contains all required fields
- Test state file is valid JSON

### State File Updates
- Test state updates as repos complete
- Test failed repos recorded with error reason
- Test timing information captured

### State File Schema
Required fields:
- started_at: ISO timestamp
- repos: array of repo states
- completed_count: number
- failed_count: number
- current_phase: string
- worker_pids: array (parallel mode)

## Resume Tests

### Basic Resume
- Test --resume detects existing state
- Test completed repos are skipped
- Test failed repos are retried
- Test progress continues from last position

### Resume After Interruption
- Simulate Ctrl+C during processing
- Verify state file is complete
- Test resume picks up correctly

### Resume Validation
- Test resume rejects corrupted state
- Test resume warns on stale state (> 24h)
- Test --restart clears state and starts fresh

## Artifact Capture Tests

### Git State Capture
- Test git_status_before captured
- Test git_status_after captured
- Test diff between states computed

### Agent Output Capture
- Test pane_tail captured
- Test commit_plan JSON saved
- Test release_plan JSON saved (if present)

### Artifact Storage
- Test artifacts stored per-repo
- Test artifact directory structure correct
- Test large outputs truncated appropriately

## Logging Requirements
- State file writes logged
- Resume decisions logged
- Artifact capture operations logged
- Any skipped repos explained

## Related Beads
- Tests: bd-hkmt (state file management)
- Tests: bd-7x6h (artifact capture)
- Parent epic: bd-a2wt (Testing Strategy)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] State file created with correct schema
- [ ] State updates atomically (no partial writes)
- [ ] Resume skips completed repos
- [ ] Resume retries failed repos
- [ ] Interrupted runs leave valid state
- [ ] Artifacts captured for each processed repo
- [ ] Large artifacts handled gracefully
- [ ] All state operations have detailed logging","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:26:55.663565669-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T17:26:55.663565669-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-taoo","depends_on_id":"bd-hkmt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.528936906-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-taoo","depends_on_id":"bd-7x6h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:37.550874918-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-taoo","depends_on_id":"bd-2ze9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:38:02.195432039-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-tcns","title":"Design agent prompts for review workflow","description":"Task: Design Agent Prompts for Review Workflow

Purpose
-------
Craft the prompts that instruct Claude on how to review issues/PRs, what
constraints to follow (no gh mutations), and what artifacts to produce.

Background: Prompt Engineering Matters
--------------------------------------
The quality of automated reviews depends heavily on prompt design:
- Clear policy communication (no PRs accepted)
- Explicit constraints (Plan mode = no mutations)
- Required output format (review-plan.json)
- Independent verification emphasis

Prompt Components
-----------------

1. System Context (AGENTS.md Reading)
   First read ALL of AGENTS.md and README.md carefully.
   Use your code investigation agent mode to fully understand the code,
   technical architecture, and purpose of the project. Use ultrathink.

2. Digest Handling
   If prior digest exists: read it, update based on delta commits
   If no digest: create comprehensive fresh digest
   Always write updated digest to .ru/repo-digest.md

3. Policy Communication
   POLICY: We do not accept PRs or outside contributions. The maintainer
   policy is disclosed to users. Claude reviews and independently decides
   whether and how to address submissions. Bug reports are welcome.

4. Review Instructions
   For each work item:
   - Read via gh issue view or gh pr view
   - Verify claims independently - do not trust user reports blindly
   - Check dates against recent commits (many issues may be stale)
   - If actionable: create local commits with fixes/features
   - If unclear: prepare question for maintainer

5. Plan Mode Constraints (CRITICAL)
   - DO NOT run gh issue comment, gh issue close, gh pr comment, etc.
   - DO NOT push any changes
   - Only use gh for READ operations (view, list)
   - All mutations will be applied by ru in a separate phase

6. Required Output
   Must produce .ru/review-plan.json with schema:
   {
     "schema_version": 1,
     "run_id": "...",
     "repo": "owner/repo",
     "items": [...],
     "questions": [...],
     "git": {...},
     "gh_actions": [...]
   }

Prompt Template Function
------------------------
generate_review_prompt() takes:
- wt_path: worktree path
- repo_name: owner/repo format
- work_items: JSON array of items to review
- run_id: unique review run ID

Checks for existing digest and adjusts instructions accordingly.

Prompt Variations
-----------------
- Quick Review: Focus on high-priority items, target 10 minutes
- Deep Review: Comprehensive analysis, architectural implications
- Security-Focused: Security items first, verify all claims

Testing
-------
- Verify Claude produces valid review-plan.json
- Verify gh mutations are NOT attempted
- Verify digest is created/updated
- Verify questions use AskUserQuestion tool

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Prompt template produces working reviews
- [ ] Plan mode constraints enforced
- [ ] Review plan artifact created correctly
- [ ] Policy communicated clearly
- [ ] Digest handling works both cases
- [ ] Questions formatted for aggregation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:37:17.313860817-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:44:47.540328214-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-tcns","depends_on_id":"bd-koxf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:54.883229859-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-tsya","title":"Fix test_unit_review cleanup + exit-code assertions","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T23:31:19.377435171-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T23:33:08.777160299-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T23:33:08.777160299-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: safer mock isolation + assert exit codes"}
 {"id":"bd-u2t8","title":"Implement release plan validation and execution","description":"Implements validate_release_plan() and execute_release_plan() functions per the NTM integration plan.

## Background

After Phase 3, the agent produces a release plan JSON. Before any release operations, ru MUST validate this plan against security rules. This mirrors the commit plan validation but handles release-specific concerns like version formats and changelog entries.

## Functions to Implement

### validate_release_plan()

Purpose: Validate the release plan JSON against safety rules before execution.

Inputs:
- plan_json: The raw release plan from the agent
- repo_path: Path to the repository

Validation Rules:
1. version must match semver format (vX.Y.Z or X.Y.Z)
2. changelog must exist and contain expected version header
3. tag_name must not already exist
4. title must be < 200 chars
5. body must be < 10000 chars
6. files array must pass file denylist check
7. No shell metacharacters in any string field

Returns: 0 if valid, non-zero with error messages on stderr if invalid.

### execute_release_plan()

Purpose: Execute a validated release plan using gh CLI and git commands.

Steps:
1. Verify plan was validated (guard against direct calls)
2. Create and push the git tag to origin (two-step: create locally, then transmit to remote)
3. Create GitHub release with gh release create
4. Upload any specified release assets
5. Capture release URL for reporting

Error Handling:
- If tag creation fails, abort and report
- If release creation fails, attempt cleanup of tag
- Log all operations for audit trail

## Implementation Notes

- Uses gh release create for GitHub release creation
- Tag transmission to remote uses standard git remote operations
- All string fields must be shell-escaped before use
- Follows the Planner->Validator->Executor pattern

## Related Beads

- Depends on: bd-8bxp (validate_commit_plan - similar pattern)
- Depends on: bd-y3vd (has_release_workflow detection)
- Parent epic: bd-jk4n (Security Guardrails)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] validate_release_plan() rejects invalid version formats
- [ ] validate_release_plan() rejects existing tags
- [ ] validate_release_plan() enforces string length limits
- [ ] execute_release_plan() creates tag and release atomically
- [ ] execute_release_plan() handles partial failures gracefully
- [ ] All operations logged for audit trail","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:21:36.975238928-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-06T17:21:36.975238928-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u2t8","depends_on_id":"bd-8bxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:19.309161247-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-u2t8","depends_on_id":"bd-y3vd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-06T17:27:19.338995792-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
